question: class Time Remember to make data members private. Members: int hour; int minute; int second; void setHour ( int h ); void setMinute ( int m ); void setSecond ( int s ); void setTime ( int h, int m, int s ); int getHour ( ); int getMinute ( ); int getSecond ( ); void printTwentyFourHourFormat(); // print universal time void printTwelveHourFormat(); // print standard time void incSec( int = 1 ); // increment in the second of the calling time object // default increment is 1 void incMin( int = 1 ); // increment in the minute of the calling time object // default increment is 1 void incHour( int = 1 ); // increment in the hour of the calling time object // default increment is 1 Input Validation: All Time object should have values of hour >= 1 and <= 23. And minute and second in range >= 1 and <= 59 Do proper validations

test_cases.expected:
Default time: 01:01:01
Parameterized time (24-hour): 14:30:45
Parameterized time (12-hour): 02:30:45 PM
After setting time: 09:15:30 AM
After incrementing seconds by 35: 09:16:05
After incrementing minutes by 50: 10:06:05
After incrementing hours by 5: 15:06:05

Default time: 01:01:01
Parameterized time (24-hour): 25:70:80
Parameterized time (12-hour): 01:70:80 PM
After setting time: -5:70:80 AM
After incrementing seconds by 35: -5:70:115
After incrementing minutes by 50: -5:120:115
After incrementing hours by 5: 00:120:115

errors: No input validation in setter functions - accepts any values including negative and out-of-range
No validation in increment functions for negative increments
No error handling for invalid time values
Program vulnerable to displaying invalid time formats
Missing bounds checking allows invalid time states
